exam.title: MSc Admission Test Preparation
instructions: Answer all fifty questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
There is no negative marking. The qualifying mark is 60 (40%).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If two nonzero vectors u and v are orthogonal, then their dot product u·v equals:
questions.explanation: Orthogonal vectors meet at 90°, and since cos 90° = 0, their dot product is 0.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Solve the system x + y = 5 and x − y = 1.
questions.explanation: Adding the equations gives 2x = 6, so x = 3; substituting back gives y = 2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is $\int \frac{1}{x}\,dx$?
questions.explanation: The antiderivative of 1/x is the natural logarithm ln|x| + C.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: How many ordered arrangements of 2 items can be chosen from 5 distinct items, i.e. P(5, 2)?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The function $f(x) = x^2$ is classified as:
questions.explanation: Since f(−x) = (−x)² = x² = f(x), the function is even (symmetric about the y-axis).

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: What is the expected value of a single roll of a fair six-sided die?

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is most strongly affected by extreme outliers?
questions.explanation: The mean uses every value, so a few extreme outliers can shift it substantially; the median resists them.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: In hypothesis testing, a Type I error occurs when one:
questions.explanation: A Type I error (false positive) is rejecting a null hypothesis that is actually true.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: The intersection of two context-free languages is:
questions.explanation: Context-free languages are not closed under intersection; e.g. {aⁿbⁿcᵐ} ∩ {aⁿbᵐcᵐ} = {aⁿbⁿcⁿ}, which is not context-free.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: A Turing machine that halts on every input is called a:
questions.explanation: A decider is a Turing machine that always halts, so it decides (rather than merely recognizes) its language.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Which of the following languages is NOT regular?
questions.explanation: {0ⁿ1ⁿ} requires counting/matching, which a finite automaton cannot do; the others are regular.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: The equivalence of NFAs and DFAs is proved using the:
questions.explanation: The subset construction builds a DFA whose states are sets of NFA states, showing the two models accept the same languages.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does this C code print?
questions.explanation: When an array is partially initialized, the remaining elements are set to 0, so arr[4] is 0.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of the following C code?
questions.explanation: The condition x > 3 is true, so the ternary returns x, which is 5.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which Java keyword prevents a class from being subclassed (inherited)?
questions.explanation: Declaring a class `final` forbids any class from extending it.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does this C loop print?
questions.explanation: The loop prints i for i = 0, 1, 2 with no separators, producing "012".

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which C++ operator dynamically allocates memory on the heap?
questions.explanation: In C++, the `new` operator allocates heap memory and calls the constructor; `malloc` is the C library function.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element of an array by its index?
questions.explanation: Array indexing computes the element's address directly, giving constant-time O(1) access.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which graph traversal naturally uses a stack (or recursion)?
questions.explanation: DFS explores as deep as possible before backtracking, which is implemented with a stack or recursion; BFS uses a queue.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which of the following is a self-balancing binary search tree?
questions.explanation: An AVL tree rebalances via rotations to keep its height O(log n); a heap is not a search tree.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Evaluating a postfix (Reverse Polish) expression is most naturally done using a:
questions.explanation: Operands are pushed onto a stack and popped when an operator is read, making the stack the natural structure.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: The recurrence T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) solves to:
questions.explanation: Halving the problem with constant work per level (as in binary search) gives O(log n).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which algorithm computes a minimum spanning tree of a weighted graph?
questions.explanation: Kruskal's algorithm builds an MST by adding the lowest-weight edges that don't form a cycle; the others compute shortest paths.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Direct Memory Access (DMA) allows:

options.a: the CPU to access cache faster
options.b: I/O devices to transfer data to/from memory without continuous CPU involvement

questions.explanation: DMA lets a controller move data between an I/O device and memory directly, freeing the CPU during the transfer.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: The program counter (PC) register holds:

options.b: the address of the next instruction to fetch
options.d: the result of the last operation

questions.explanation: The PC stores the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched and executed.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: How many 1-bits are in the binary representation of the decimal number 7?
questions.explanation: 7 in binary is 111, which contains three 1-bits.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A cache hit ratio of 0.9 means that:

options.b: 90% of memory accesses are served from the cache

questions.explanation: Hit ratio is the fraction of accesses found in cache; 0.9 means 90% are hits (10% misses).

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Virtual memory primarily enables:

options.b: execution of processes whose total size may exceed physical memory

questions.explanation: Virtual memory uses disk as an extension of RAM, letting programs larger than physical memory run.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: The dispatcher module of an operating system is responsible for:

options.a: choosing which process should run next
options.b: giving CPU control to the process the scheduler selected (the context switch)

questions.explanation: The scheduler picks the next process; the dispatcher actually hands it the CPU via a context switch.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which page-replacement algorithm can exhibit Belady's anomaly?
questions.explanation: FIFO can produce more page faults when given more frames — Belady's anomaly; stack algorithms like LRU cannot.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: A critical section is:

options.a: the boot sector of the disk
options.b: a code region accessing shared resources that must run with mutual exclusion

questions.explanation: A critical section manipulates shared data, so only one process may execute it at a time to avoid race conditions.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Shortest Job First (SJF) scheduling is provably optimal for minimizing:
questions.explanation: SJF minimizes average waiting time by always running the shortest available job first.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which TCP/IP layer is responsible for end-to-end, reliable delivery between hosts?
questions.explanation: The transport layer (e.g. TCP) provides end-to-end reliability, sequencing, and flow control.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: In classful addressing, the IP address 192.168.1.1 belongs to which class?
questions.explanation: Addresses with a first octet from 192–223 are Class C; 192 falls in this range.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which port does HTTPS use by default?
questions.explanation: HTTPS (HTTP over TLS) uses TCP port 443 by default.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which protocol automatically assigns IP addresses to hosts on a network?
questions.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) leases IP addresses and other parameters to hosts automatically.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: A view in SQL is:

options.a: a physical copy of table data
options.b: a virtual table defined by a stored query

questions.explanation: A view is a named, virtual table whose contents are produced by running its defining query when accessed.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which of the following is a Data Definition Language (DDL) command?
questions.explanation: CREATE defines schema objects (DDL); SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE manipulate data (DML).

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: The primary goal of database normalization is to:

options.b: reduce redundancy and avoid update anomalies

questions.explanation: Normalization organizes data to minimize redundancy and the insertion/update/deletion anomalies it causes.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: A deadlock between two transactions is typically resolved by:

options.b: rolling back one transaction chosen as the victim

questions.explanation: The DBMS breaks the cycle by aborting and rolling back a victim transaction, releasing its locks.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: Sharding a database means:

options.a: keeping a full copy of the data on every node
options.b: horizontally partitioning the data across multiple servers

questions.explanation: Sharding splits rows of a dataset across nodes (horizontal partitioning) so each node holds a subset.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: During a network partition, the CAP property that must be sacrificed if availability is kept is:
questions.explanation: CAP says under a partition you choose between consistency and availability; keeping availability sacrifices strong consistency.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: A heartbeat message in a distributed system is used primarily to:

options.b: detect whether a node is still alive

questions.explanation: Periodic heartbeats let peers detect node/link failures when expected messages stop arriving.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Depth-first search is not guaranteed to find a solution when:

options.b: the state space can be infinitely deep

questions.explanation: DFS can descend an infinite branch forever, so it is not complete in infinite-depth spaces.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: A confusion matrix is used to evaluate the performance of a:
questions.explanation: A confusion matrix tabulates true/false positives and negatives, summarizing classification accuracy.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Which regularization technique reduces overfitting by randomly deactivating neurons during training?
questions.explanation: Dropout randomly omits a fraction of neurons each step, preventing co-adaptation and reducing overfitting.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: The Turing Test evaluates a machine's ability to:

options.b: carry on a conversation indistinguishable from a human

questions.explanation: In the Turing Test, a machine passes if a human interrogator cannot reliably tell it apart from a human in conversation.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: If today is Wednesday, what day of the week will it be 100 days from now?
questions.explanation: 100 mod 7 = 2, so the day advances by 2 from Wednesday, giving Friday.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
questions.explanation: Each term is the previous one doubled plus one (×2 + 1): 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: In a certain code, 'MAN' is written as 'NBO'. How is 'CAT' written in the same code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one: C→D, A→B, T→U, giving 'DBU'.
